figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic of the separated flow field downstream of reflected shock in nominally 2-dimensional SBLI (adapted from Piponniau et al. (2009))

Figure 2. (a) Ensemble-averaged pressure distribution; pressure-time variation for upstream shock foot movement at (b) shock oscillation region (SOR); (c) plateau pressure region (PPR); (d) rising pressure region (RPR) (adapted from Brusniak & Dolling (1994))
Figure 3. Schematic of (a) experimental setup. (b) different protuberances considered in the experiments.
Figure 4. Fluctuating data matrix construction in snapshot POD

Figure 5. Mean flow fields of the interaction (a) oilflow streakline patterns (b) surface pressure from RANS simulations (Bhardwaj et al. 2022); FS - freestream, MS - mean shock foot, PP - plateau pressure zone, LP - low pressure zone, HP - high pressure zone
Figure 7. Separation shock unsteady characteristics along the scan line (a) space-time (x-t) variation (b) power spectral density.
Figure 8. Centerline span POD of cubical protuberance (a) spatial modes. (b) PSD of corresponding temporal coefficients
Figure 9. Schematic of transducer locations strategized for the pressure measurements
Figure 11. Statistical relations of signals between mean shock and low-pressure zone for cubical protuberance (a) cross correlation. (b) coherence.
Figure 13. Mean shock correlations (a) short cylinder (b) cuboid
Figure 14. Streaklines affecting different regions of the interaction.
Figure 15. Mean shock and high pressure (centerline) correlations (a) short cylinder (b) cuboid
Figure 16. Shock oscillation characteristics (a) frequency scaling based on separation length applied to various protuberances. (b) root-mean-square of shock oscillations with separation extent.
Figure 17. Computational domain with boundary conditions. Numerical schlieren on slices (𝑦/𝛿 = 0, 𝑧/𝛿 = 0) are shown to illustrate the essential instantaneous flow features.
Figure 18. Mean flow patterns of (a) streamwise velocity (b) streamlines originating at mid-span from the upstream (inflow) boundary.
Figure 19. Contours of surface pressure. Distinct pressure zones of the interaction are marked.
Figure 20. Fields of 𝐿𝑅 representing RANS (𝐿𝑅 = 0, 𝑏𝑙𝑢𝑒) and LES (𝐿𝑅 = 1, 𝑟𝑒𝑑) zones extracted along 𝑦/𝛿 = 0.3 and 𝑧/𝛿 = 6.0
Figure 21. Iso-surfaces of Q-criterion (Q = 0.1) coloured with streamwise velocity. A numerical schlieren on a slice (𝑧/𝛿 = 0) is also included, where |∇𝜌 |∗ = 0.80 × 𝑒−15 |∇𝜌 |/|∇𝜌 |𝑚𝑎𝑥 .
Figure 22. Topology of the mean flow (a) surface streaklines (R1) (b) oil flow visualization (experiments) (c) surface streaklines (R2)
Figure 23. Comparison of mean surface pressure distributions at various span locations: filled circle - experiments; solid line - computations (R1); dotted line - computations (R2).

Figure 24. Instantaneous numerical schlieren snapshots illustrating the temporal excursions of the separation shock along the centerline span. Three dotted lines denote the upstream-most, approximate mean, and downstream-most positions of the shock foot, respectively.
Figure 25. Centerline shock characteristics (a) space-time (x-t) variation (b) standard deviation of pixel intensities in numerical schlieren.

Figure 26. Distribution of data probes in the interaction zone ( MS - mean shock, PP - plateau pressure, LP - low pressure, HP - high pressure, 𝑅𝐴𝐸 - reattachment (experiments), 𝑅𝐴𝐶 - reattachment (computations) )
Figure 27. Power spectral densities of probes along the centerline span (a) R1 SPSW (b) R2 SPOF
Figure 28. Statistical relations between mean shock and other locations (a) cross correlation. (b) coherence.
Figure 29. Power spectral densities of probes along the mean shock (a) R1 SPSW (b) R2 SPOF
Figure 30. Statistical relations among probes along the mean shock foot (a) cross correlation. (b) coherence.
Figure 31. Instantaneous picture of spanwise shock motion at different instances. The dotted lines represent upstream-most and downstream-most positions corresponding to three cycles.
Figure 32. Distribution of (a) mean streamwise velocity gradient at the wall (dotted curve indicates the separated zone). (b) Intermittency (𝛾) of separation shock
Figure 33. POD based on wall pressure corresponding to mode 1, 2 and 7 (R1 SPSW). (a) spatial mode. (b) PSD of temporal coefficients of the respective mode
Figure 34. POD based on wall pressure (R2 SPOF). (a) spatial mode. (b) PSD of temporal coefficients
Figure 35. Three-dimensional POD: (a) spatial mode (b) PSD
Figure 36. Reconstructed flow field with low-frequency modes.
Figure 37. Evolution of reconstructed flow field (continued). (a) Q-criterion. (b) streamwise velocity at 𝑦/𝛿 = 0.4.
Figure 38. A streamline in the mean flow field representing the mean path of a particle moving along the separated shear layer.
